$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the INTERNAL bridge protocol version (MCP server <-> native host <-> extension). This is NOT the MCP JSON-RPC version (that is the date string '2025-06-18', see docs/adr/0007) and NOT the extension release version (Cargo is the release version source). It is a small monotonically-increasing integer, bumped only when the bridge wire contract (BridgeReq/BridgeResp shape, hello handshake, op/capability semantics) changes incompatibly.

handshake.$comment: Intended compatibility handshake, layered on top of the existing hello authentication (see docs/adr/0002). Design only; see docs/rfc/0001-connection-state-machine.md and docs/architecture.md.
handshake.flow: On connect, the native host -> MCP server exchange carries {hello, protocolVersion, capabilities[]}: after the secret is validated, the extension advertises its available capability ids (from capabilities.json) and its protocolVersion. The server compares protocolVersion and the capability set against what it supports.
handshake.onMismatch: Fail fast: if protocolVersion is incompatible, the server rejects the connection with the PROTOCOL_MISMATCH error (see contracts/errors.json) and a clear message, instead of accepting the connection and only later surfacing a confusing 'unknown op' when a newer/older op is dispatched.
handshake.onMissingCapability: If a tool's required capability (per capabilities.json) is not advertised, the server rejects that tool call up front with a clear message rather than dispatching an op the extension cannot handle.
